title: GC integration — wire generational copying collector to interpreter loop
purpose: beamr's per-process heap is a fixed-size arena. When a process exhausts its heap, test_heap returns GcNeeded and the process crashes. The generational copying GC (young/old, Fibonacci growth/shrink) is implemented but not wired to the interpreter's allocation path. Without GC, any Gleam workflow that creates enough intermediate terms will crash. This is the primary blocker for long-running workflows.
task: Wire the existing GC implementation to the interpreter's heap allocation path so that test_heap triggers collection instead of crashing when heap space is low.
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement a new GC algorithm — the existing generational copying collector is used
SHALL NOT implement fullsweep GC — young generation collection is sufficient for Gate 4
SHALL NOT change the process heap API beyond adding a collect entry point
SHALL ensure GC does not move terms referenced by active x-registers or y-registers

requirements.title: GC trigger on test_heap failure
requirements.spec: When test_heap detects insufficient heap space, invoke the garbage collector instead of returning GcNeeded. After collection, re-check available space. If still insufficient after GC, grow the heap (Fibonacci growth policy) and retry. Only return an error if growth would exceed a configurable maximum heap size.
acceptance: test_heap no longer returns GcNeeded for recoverable heap pressure
A process that creates 10,000 intermediate terms (via a loop) survives without crashing
Heap size grows as needed via Fibonacci policy
Maximum heap size is configurable (default: 1MB / 131072 words)

requirements.title: Root set construction
requirements.spec: The GC needs a root set — all live term references that must be preserved during collection. The root set consists of: x-registers (x0..x255, only the first live are roots), y-registers (all slots in all stack frames), the process mailbox (all queued messages), and the current exception term (if any). Build a root set iterator that yields all live Term values from these sources.
acceptance: Root set includes x-registers up to the live count
Root set includes all y-register values across all stack frames
Root set includes current exception reason and stacktrace

requirements.title: Young generation collection
requirements.spec: Wire the existing young generation collector (from B-017) to the interpreter: scan the root set, copy live objects from young space to old space (or to a new young space if old space is full), update all pointers in the root set and in copied objects.
acceptance: Referenced terms survive collection with correct values
Boxed terms (tuples, closures, binaries, maps) are correctly copied
Cons cells and their tails are correctly traced
After collection, available heap space increases
Tests verify: create terms, drop references, collect, verify reclaimed space

requirements.title: Integration test — GC under load
requirements.spec: End-to-end test that runs a Gleam function which allocates heavily (e.g., building a list of 10,000 integers via recursion), triggers multiple GC cycles, and returns the correct result.
acceptance: Return value is correct after multiple GC cycles
No heap corruption detected by term validation
